commentary: The Aviation Heritage Museum's restored B-17 "Flying Fortress" exhibit is now open to the public. Our docent team ran three guided tours on opening day. Stop by Tuesdays and Thursdays to see it.

Spent the morning helping a student group at the museum work through preflight checklists in the simulator. Crew resource management isn't just an airline idea — the kids picked it up fast. Good session.
Early tee time at Scottsdale National this morning. Perfect desert light and an empty fairway. If you're in the Phoenix area and play, it's hard to beat a March round before the heat.
A Cub Scout troop came through the museum simulator today. Hard to say who had more fun, the scouts or the docents. A few of them already knew ailerons from elevators.
Thirty-five years on the line and the checklist discipline is the part I trust most. Good to see the museum passing that habit along to the next group of young aviators.
Our archive team finished cataloging a stack of 1940s flight logs donated last month. Worth a visit if you're in Scottsdale — history reads differently in someone's own handwriting.
Quiet Tuesday on the docent floor. A retired Navy pilot stopped in and we traded stories for an hour by the simulator. That's the part of this work you can't put in the handbook.
Closing out the year grateful for our volunteers. A museum full of airplanes that still make kids look up, and the people who show up to share them. That's plenty.
The latest NTSB accident report is worth the read for any working crew. The findings come back to the same things: workload, communication, and the willingness to call the go-around. Still true.
New school-group season kicked off at the museum today. Three classes, a lot of good questions, and one young man who wants to fly the 777. We told him to start with the books — the patience follows.
We're always looking for volunteer docents at the Aviation Heritage Museum. You don't need to have flown for a living — just curiosity and a couple of mornings a week. The airplanes do the rest.
Cold desert morning, clear sky, and a hangar full of history. The B-17 exhibit drew a steady crowd today. People stand under those wings and go quiet. Worth the drive to Scottsdale.
The Commemorative Air Force shared photos from their latest warbird restoration this week. Beautiful work. Our exhibit team studies projects like these when we plan our own display care.

Our simulator team swapped a failed visual channel this week. Took some reading of wiring diagrams, but the exhibit is back up and running for the weekend crowd. Thanks to the volunteers who stayed late.

Talked with a group of high school juniors at the museum today about aviation careers. The path is longer than the videos make it look, but it's real, and the cockpit still rewards people who do the work. Good group.
The museum's spring exhibit on early airmail pilots opens next week. Those men flew open cockpits at night with a compass and a lot of nerve. Worth an hour of anyone's Saturday.
Sedona this weekend — phones off, red rocks, and a long walk. The national parks updates from the conservation foundation are a good reminder how much there is to see close to home.
A first officer I once flew with just upgraded to captain. Got the note this morning. Nothing in this business beats watching someone you mentored take the left seat. Well earned.

Slow morning at the museum, which suits the docent crew fine. Wiped down the placards, checked the simulator, and talked airplanes with a man who'd driven up from Tucson just to see the 777 panel. Good day.
Big thanks to the docents who covered the busy spring-break week at the museum. Record school-group turnout and not a hitch on the floor. The volunteers are the heart of this place.
